questions.objective_ref: Define three distinct types of AI hallucination — factual, attributional, and faithful-but-wrong — and give a concrete example of each.
Explain why the token-prediction mechanism makes hallucination a structural property of LLMs, not a bug that can be patched away.
Describe two contributing causes — training data gaps and RLHF incentives — that make hallucination more likely in practice.
Identify real-world contexts (legal, medical, academic) where hallucination has caused documented harm.
List three detection and mitigation strategies (verification, grounding, RAG) that reduce hallucination risk without eliminating it.
